platforms.name: OpenClaw
platforms.best_for: Non-technical builders who want an agent that does real work — research, writing, content, automation, browsing
platforms.cost: Free tier available. Paid plans for heavier use. AI brain cost is separate.
platforms.honest_take: The most complete agent platform right now. Learning curve is real but manageable. The community is active and helpful. Vivienne lives here — so we're biased, but we're biased because it works.

platforms.name: Claude Code
platforms.best_for: Developers, people learning to code, and anyone who wants to build software with AI help
platforms.cost: Pay per use based on tokens. Can get expensive on long sessions.
platforms.honest_take: The best coding agent available right now. It understands entire codebases, not just single files. This entire website was built with it. But it's specialized — if you don't code, this isn't your starting point.

platforms.name: Cursor
platforms.best_for: People who want to code but prefer a visual editor over a terminal
platforms.cost: Free tier with limited AI usage. Pro plan around $20/month for unlimited.
platforms.honest_take: Easier to start with than Claude Code if you're new to coding. The visual interface is less intimidating. But for complex, multi-file projects, Claude Code goes deeper. Many developers use both.

platforms.name: Replit
platforms.best_for: Quick prototypes, learning to code, and anyone who doesn't want to install anything
platforms.cost: Free tier for basics. Paid plans for more compute, storage, and AI usage.
platforms.honest_take: The fastest path from zero to 'I built something.' No setup friction at all. But for serious projects, you'll eventually want a local development setup. Think of Replit as the training wheels — good training wheels.

platforms.name: v0.dev
platforms.best_for: Generating UI components quickly — landing pages, dashboards, forms
platforms.cost: Free tier with limited generations. Paid for more.
platforms.honest_take: Amazing for getting a first draft of any UI fast. Not a full agent — it generates code, it doesn't run your business. Use it as a starting tool, not a finish line. Best paired with Claude Code or Cursor for the rest of the build.

platforms.name: Claude.ai
platforms.best_for: Careful, nuanced work — writing, analysis, thoughtful conversations
platforms.cost: Free tier available. Claude Pro around $20/month.
platforms.honest_take: Best for careful, nuanced work. Longer, more thoughtful responses. Our personal favorite for writing and analysis. Not an agent platform — it's a conversation. Great for getting started with AI, but you'll eventually move to OpenClaw or similar for real agent work.

platforms.name: ChatGPT
platforms.best_for: General-purpose AI conversations, broadest plugin ecosystem
platforms.cost: Free tier available. Plus plan around $20/month.
platforms.honest_take: Most popular. Good all-rounder. Best plugin ecosystem. Not an agent platform — it's a conversation. Start here if you've never used AI before, then graduate to agent platforms when you're ready.

platforms.name: Gemini
platforms.best_for: Multimodal tasks (images, video, docs) and Google Workspace integration
platforms.cost: Free with Google account. Paid plans for advanced features.
platforms.honest_take: Free with Google account. Good for multimodal tasks. Integrates with Google Workspace. Like the others, it's a conversation, not an agent platform — but a solid free starting point.

note: No affiliate links. No sponsored placements. Just honest recommendations.
